creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
I've tested Conrad's v2 patch and can confirm it works.  Note that we had a related issue fixed in HEAD but not in stable/10 until today (r273232).
https://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/base/273232

stable/10 prior to r273232:
> stderr: /usr/bin/ld: G: invalid DSO for symbol `pthread_create@@FBSD_1.0' definition
> //lib/libthr.so.3: could not read symbols: Bad value

stable/10 after r273232:
stderr: /usr/bin/ld: //lib/libthr.so.3: invalid DSO for symbol `pthread_create@@FBSD_1.0' definition
//lib/libthr.so.3: could not read symbols: Bad value

stable/10 with v2 patch:
stderr: /usr/bin/ld: undefined reference to symbol `pthread_create@@FBSD_1.0' (try adding -lthr)
//lib/libthr.so.3: could not read symbols: Bad value

Conrad, to be clear, you mention that this was fixed in the GPLv3 version, but your implementation here is developed independently, correct?
